title: NASA Whistleblower: “We Systematically Suppress UFO Data!”
summary: Kevin Knuth, a physicist at the University of Albany with a NASA background, discusses his research into UFO detection and propulsion physics. He shares how he became interested in UFOs after learning about nuclear missile site incursions at Malmstrom Air Force Base, discusses the physics challenges of observed UFO maneuvers, and reveals that colleagues are analyzing purported UFO crash debris using neutron activation techniques.

acronyms.definition: Security screening program for personnel working with nuclear weapons

events.description: Between September 1966 and March 1967, 30 nuclear missiles were reportedly lost to UFO activity at Malmstrom Air Force Base
Star Wars and Close Encounters of the Third Kind films released
Kevin Knuth first heard about UFO incursions at Malmstrom Air Force Base while starting graduate school at Montana State University
Robert Hastings held a news conference with six Air Force personnel discussing UFOs at nuclear sites
Fukushima nuclear disaster in Japan with reported UFO sightings
Kevin Knuth rediscovered the Malmstrom UFO story while preparing for an astronomy class
New York Times article on UFOs published

key_claims.claim: UFOs have been shutting down nuclear missiles at Malmstrom Air Force Base since at least the 1960s and this continued through the early 1990s
30 missiles were lost to UFO activity between September 1966 and March 1967
The tic-tac UFO from the Nimitz encounter accelerated at approximately 5,000 gs, requiring around 1,000 gigawatts of power
An 800-foot long USO followed the HMNZS Southland in the 1980s, closing 2 km in 25 seconds and draining the ship's batteries
Kevin Knuth has met people who claimed to have worked on or seen recovered UFO craft and found them entirely believable
Those who claimed to work on recovered craft said they don't know what anything is or how anything works
Matthew Szydagis is analyzing purported UFO crash debris using neutron activation techniques
Some of the debris being analyzed includes Art's Parts material from Roswell
A NASA employee wrote a CIA training manual for remote viewing
NASA has a 2003 patent on a barrel-shaped asymmetric capacitor referencing Townsend Brown's work
Alan Bean from Apollo 12 photographed a red flashing light during Skylab mission
Astronaut Story Musgrave reported seeing snake-like things writhing around in space
Friends who worked on anti-gravity research received threats
